insight: P2's 2.da and 2.ddc openings are among the best for low and mid economy games, while P1 benefits more in higher economy games with openings like 3.ddb, 3.dda, and 4.dddb.
author: p0lari
context: p0lari was advising a player on how P1 and P2 differ in opening theory and which openings are efficient for each side.

insight: When learning the game, picking a simple plan (such as fast Animus) and sticking to it is more effective than trying to adapt to complex situations mid-game, especially under time pressure.
author: p0lari
context: p0lari was advising a newer player on how to approach ladder matches, recommending a straightforward aggressive strategy.

insight: A newly released unit (referred to as Valkyrion) synergizes strongly with itself at 4 supply, making it a near-must-buy in most sets. Experts discussed reducing its supply to 1, increasing its cost to 2B, or lowering its health as potential fixes.
author: kristian1103
context: Community discussion immediately after a patch introduced Valkyrion, with Arkanishu and masn6811 debating its power level and possible balance adjustments.

insight: Buying four of the newly released unit (Valkyrion) and holding them every other turn effectively creates a 4/0/4 attacker that absorbs on off-turns, functioning similarly to two Iso Kronuses with Infusion Grid on the off-turn.
author: kristian1103
context: Arkanishu and masn6811 were analyzing the self-synergy of Valkyrion when stacked at 4 supply and alternating attack and absorb turns.

insight: A newly released unit (Thermite Core) was considered potentially too strong, with a cost change to 2B discussed as a reasonable adjustment. Its 0 absorb for the first ~14 turns after the patch was also criticized as making it feel unplayable early.
author: kristian1103
context: Players were reacting to the new patch that introduced Thermite Core, debating its power and a hotfix's change to its absorb timing.

insight: The random unit pool for non-base units has no restrictions on color or type ratios, meaning a game can legitimately produce sets that are all green, all blockers, etc.
author: redrame
context: A new player asked whether there are minimum color or type ratio requirements for the random unit set, and redrame and jeacaveo both confirmed there are none.
